html:
[1474]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 飛益惶惧 - Fei became increasingly fearful.
  • 反大声呼铜问之 - He called out loudly to Tong and asked him.
  • 答曰 - He replied.
  • 来者是水边穴中白鼍 - The one who came is a white alligator from a hole by the water.
  • 我是庙北岩嵌中龟也 - I am a turtle in the crevice of the rock north of the temple.
  • 非迨旦而告里人 - Fei waited until dawn to tell the villagers.
  • 共往发掘 - They went together to dig it up.
  • 杀之 - Killed it.
  • 遂坏庙绝祀 - And so the temple was destroyed and the worship ceased.
  • 民始得安堵焉 - The people finally found peace.
  • 近世波斯人 - A Persian from recent times.
  • 舟漂入大海 - His boat drifted into the sea.
  • 至一岛中 - He arrived at an island.
  • 见衣草叶胡人 - He saw a man dressed in grass and leaves.
  • 问之 - He asked him.
  • 乃知亦是失风漂流者 - He learned that he was also a person who had been lost to the wind and drifted.
  • 众哀而载之 - The crowd pitied him and took him on board.
  • 胡即日献山中磲、玛瑙、玻璃等诸宝 - The man immediately offered various treasures such as shells, agate, and glass from the mountain.
  • 不可胜数 - Too numerous to count.
  • 舟人都争取之 - The crew all took some of them.
  • 满载挂帆 - The boat was fully loaded and set sail.
  • 行舟可三四十里 - The boat traveled about thirty to forty li.
  • 遥见山峰有赤物如蛇 - From afar, they saw a red object like a snake on a mountain peak.
  • 渐至巨大 - Gradually becoming huge.
  • 胡曰 - The man said.
  • 山神惜宝，来逐我也 - The mountain god is jealous of the treasures and is chasing me.
  • 为之奈何 - What should I do?
  • 舟人无不战惧 - The crew were all terrified.
  • 俄见两山从海中出 - Soon, they saw two mountains emerging from the sea.
  • 高数百丈 - Hundreds of zhang high.
  • 胡喜曰 - The man happily said.
  • 此两山者，大蟹鳌也 - These two mountains are the claws of a giant crab.
  • 其蟹常好与山神斗 - The crab often likes to fight with the mountain god.
  • 神多不胜 - The god often loses.
  • 我等必无忧矣 - We will surely have no worries.
  • 赤物寻至 - The red object soon arrived.
  • 两山盘斗 - The two mountains fought.
  • 良久 - For a long time.
  • 见蟹夹蛇头 - They saw the crab grab the snake's head.
  • 死于水上 - Died in the water.
  • 状如连山 - It looked like two connected mountains.
  • 舟乃得渡 - The boat was then able to cross.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 飞 - Fei, a character in the story.
  • 铜 - Tong, another character in the story.
  • 白鼍 - A white alligator, a mythical creature in Chinese folklore.
  • 龟 - A turtle, often associated with longevity and wisdom in Chinese culture.
  • 庙 - A temple, a place of worship.
  • 岩嵌 - A crevice in a rock, often associated with hidden or sacred places.
  • 波斯人 - A Persian, a person from Persia, a historical region in modern-day Iran.
  • 山中磲、玛瑙、玻璃 - Shells, agate, and glass from the mountain.

[1475]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 乾道間 - Refers to the period of the reign of Emperor Gaozong of Song, specifically during the years 1165-1173.
  • 掃墓 - A common activity during Qingming Festival, involving cleaning and honoring ancestral graves.
  • 分庭抗禮 - A formal greeting where both parties stand side by side, indicating equal status.
  • 累劫同修 - Refers to a long history of shared cultivation or practice, often used in Buddhist contexts.
  • 冥數未合 - Suggests that their fates are not yet aligned or destined to be together.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 歷陽芮不疑 - The name of the protagonist, with '芮不疑' being his personal name.
  • 青衣小鬟 - Refers to a young girl dressed in simple clothing, likely a servant or attendant.

Proper Nouns

  • 持簡邀之 - '持簡' means holding a letter or document, and '邀之' means inviting him.
  • 赫然華屋 - Describes a grand and impressive house.

[1476]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 奇遇 - Refers to an extraordinary or unusual encounter.
  • 幻化 - Suggests that the food or drink might be transformed into something else.
  • 食之疾矣 - Means that eating it could make Rui sick.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 蒸羊 - A traditional Chinese dish, steamed lamb, often associated with special occasions and delicacies.
  • 丽人 - Refers to a beautiful woman, possibly a supernatural or mystical figure.

Proper Nouns

  • 芮 - A personal name.
  • 丽人 - A personal name or title, referring to a beautiful and wise woman.

[1477]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 适值 - Means 'happened to encounter' or 'just happened to meet.'
  • 屈道人 - Refers to a Daoist practitioner or hermit named Qu.
  • 自称精于天心法 - Means 'claimed to be proficient in the Tianxin method,' where 'Tianxin' refers to a Daoist technique or ritual.
  • 父备白其故 - Means 'the father told him the reason,' where '父备' refers to the father being prepared or ready to explain.
  • 岛洞列仙 - Refers to 'island caves and celestial immortals,' a metaphor for Daoist practitioners living in remote, mystical places.
  • 为淫佚之行 - Means 'engaged in licentious behavior,' where '淫佚' refers to immoral or excessive conduct.
  • 吾能治之，况于他乎 - Means 'I can handle it, let alone others,' expressing confidence in dealing with the situation.
  • 遂索线十丈 - Means 'then he asked for ten zhang of thread,' where '线' is thread and '十丈' is ten zhang (a unit of length).
  • 以针贯小符于抄 - Means 'and threaded a small talisman with a needle onto a piece of paper,' where '针' is a needle, '贯' is to thread, '小符' is a small talisman, and '抄' is a piece of paper.
  • 藏诸合中 - Means 'and hid it in a box,' where '合' is a box or container.
  • 祝芮曰 - Means 'he said to Rui,' where '祝' is to say or speak.
  • 君甘妖惑 - Means 'you willingly indulge in witchcraft,' where '甘' means to willingly indulge and '妖惑' means witchcraft or sorcery.
  • 有死而已 - Means 'you will die, that's all,' expressing a dire consequence.
  • 如未甘死 - Means 'if you do not want to die,' where '未甘' means not willing.
  • 俟彼去时 - Means 'wait until he leaves,' where '俟' means to wait and '彼' means he or she.
  • 将此符粘于衣裙 - Means 'attach this talisman to your clothes,' where '粘' means to attach and '衣裙' means clothes or garments.
  • 任其带线而去 - Means 'let him take the thread with him,' where '带' means to take or carry and '线' is thread.
  • 彼若正神明 - Means 'if he is a true deity,' where '正' means true or genuine and '神明' means deity or god.
  • 无妨也 - Means 'it will not be a problem,' expressing a lack of concern.
  • 聊资一笑之适 - Means 'just for a laugh,' where '聊' means just or merely, '资' means to provide, '一笑' means a laugh, and '适' means pleasure or enjoyment.
  • 明日 - Means 'the next day.'
  • 屈先生遍访野外 - Means 'Mr. Qu searched the wilderness,' where '遍访' means to search thoroughly and '野外' means the wilderness.
  • 有一巨蟒死焉 - Means 'a giant python was found dead there,' where '巨蟒' is a giant python and '死焉' means was found dead there.
  • 尸横百尺 - Means 'the body was a hundred chi long,' where '尸横' means the body lying and '百尺' is a hundred chi (a unit of length).
  • 其符在鳞甲可见也 - Means 'the talisman was visible on its scales,' where '其符' is the talisman, '在鳞甲' means on its scales, and '可见' means visible.
  • 芮始醒焉如醉 - Means 'Rui finally woke up from his drunken state,' where '始醒' means finally woke up and '如醉' means like being drunk.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 屈道人 - A Daoist practitioner or hermit named Qu, who is said to be proficient in the Tianxin method, a Daoist technique or ritual.
  • 天心法 - A Daoist method or ritual, possibly related to exorcism or spiritual healing.
  • 岛洞列仙 - A metaphor for Daoist practitioners living in remote, mystical places, often associated with immortality and supernatural powers.
  • 淫佚之行 - Immoral or excessive behavior, often associated with witchcraft or sorcery in Daoist and Confucian texts.
  • 小符 - A small talisman, often used in Daoist rituals for protection or exorcism.
  • 正神明 - A true deity, often associated with Daoist deities or immortals.

Proper Nouns

  • 屈道人 - A Daoist practitioner or hermit named Qu.
  • 天心法 - A Daoist method or ritual, possibly related to exorcism or spiritual healing.
  • 岛洞列仙 - A metaphor for Daoist practitioners living in remote, mystical places.
  • 小符 - A small talisman, often used in Daoist rituals for protection or exorcism.
  • 正神明 - A true deity, often associated with Daoist deities or immortals.

[1478]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 度自御史罢归河东 - 度 is the subject, 御史 refers to an official position, 罢归 means to be dismissed and return home, and 河东 is a region in Shanxi Province.
  • 适遇侯生卒 - 适遇 means to happen to meet or encounter, and 侯生卒 indicates that a person named 侯生 has died.
  • 得此镜 - 得 means to obtain, and 此镜 refers to a specific mirror.
  • 至其年六月 - Indicates the time of June in the same year.
  • 度归长安 - 度 is the subject, 归 means to return, and 长安 is the capital city of the Sui Dynasty, now known as Xi'an.
  • 至长乐坡，宿于主人程雄家 - 至 means to arrive at, 长乐坡 is a place name, 宿 means to stay overnight, and 主人程雄家 refers to the host named 程雄 and his family.
  • 雄新受寄一婢 - 雄 is the subject, 新受寄 means to have recently received on trust, and 一婢 refers to a female servant.
  • 颇甚端丽 - 颇甚 means very much, and 端丽 describes the servant as beautiful and graceful.
  • 名曰鹦鹉 - 名曰 means called or named, and 鹦鹉 is the name of the servant.
  • 度既税驾 - 度 is the subject, 税驾 means to stop or halt one's carriage.
  • 将整冠履 - 将 means to prepare, and 整冠履 refers to adjusting one's hat and shoes.
  • 引镜自照 - 引 means to take or draw, 镜 is the mirror, and 自照 means to look at oneself in the mirror.
  • 鹦鹉遥见 - 鹦鹉 is the subject, 遥见 means to see from a distance.
  • 即便叩首流血 - 即便 means immediately, 叩首 means to bow one's head in reverence, and 流血 indicates that the servant was bleeding.
  • 云“不敢住” - 云 means to say, and 不敢住 means not to stay or not to remain.
  • 度疑精魅 - 度 is the subject, 疑 means to suspect, and 精魅 refers to a spirit or demon.
  • 引镜逼之 - 引 means to take or draw, 镜 is the mirror, and 逼之 means to force or press upon her.
  • 便云“乞命，将变形” - 便云 means to say immediately, 乞命 means to beg for life, and 将变形 means to transform.
  • 度即掩镜曰 - 度 is the subject, 即 means immediately, 掩镜 means to cover the mirror, and 曰 means to say.
  • 汝先自叙，然后变形，当舍汝命 - 汝 means you, 先自叙 means to first tell your story, 然后 means then, 变形 means to transform, and 当舍汝命 means to spare your life.
  • 婢再拜 - 婢 is the subject, 再拜 means to bow twice in reverence.
  • 自陈云 - 自陈 means to state oneself, and 云 means to say.
  • 某是华山府君庙前长松下千年老狸 - 某 means I, 是 means am, 华山府君庙 refers to the temple of the Lord of Mount Hua, 长松下 means under a tall pine tree, and 千年老狸 means a thousand-year-old fox.
  • 大行变惑，罪合至死 - 大行变惑 means to greatly transform and bewitch, 罪合至死 means the crime deserves death.
  • 遂为府君捕逐 - 遂 means then, 为 means to be, 府君 refers to the Lord of Mount Hua, 捕逐 means to chase and capture.
  • 逃于河渭之间 - 逃 means to flee, 于 means in, 河渭之间 refers to the area between the Yellow River and the Wei River.
  • 为下陈思恭义女 - 为 means to be, 下 means lower class, 陈思恭 is a person's name, and 义女 means adopted daughter.
  • 蒙养甚厚 - 蒙 means to receive, 养 means to be raised, and 甚厚 means very well.
  • 嫁与同乡人柴华 - 嫁 means to marry, 与 means to, 同乡人 means a fellow villager, and 柴华 is a person's name.
  • 华意不惬 - 华 is the subject, 意 means intention or feeling, and 不惬 means not satisfied.
  • 逃而东出韩城县 - 逃 means to flee, 而 means and, 东出 means to go eastward, and 韩城县 is a place name.
  • 为行人李无傲所执 - 为 means to be, 行人 means a messenger or traveler, 李无傲 is a person's name, and 所执 means to be captured.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 大业七年 - The seventh year of the Daiye era, which was during the reign of Emperor Yang of Sui (581-618 AD).
  • 御史 - An official position in the Sui Dynasty responsible for oversight and inspection.
  • 河东 - A historical region in northern China, corresponding to modern-day Shanxi Province.
  • 长安 - The capital city of the Sui Dynasty, now known as Xi'an.
  • 长乐坡 - A specific location in the vicinity of Chang'an.
  • 华山府君庙 - A temple dedicated to the Lord of Mount Hua, a deity associated with Mount Hua in Shaanxi Province.
  • 千年老狸 - A thousand-year-old fox, which in Chinese folklore can transform into a human and is often associated with supernatural abilities.
  • 河渭之间 - The area between the Yellow River and the Wei River, important regions in ancient China.
  • 韩城县 - A historical county in Shaanxi Province, now part of Weinan City.

Proper Nouns

  • 大业七年 - The seventh year of the Daiye era.
  • 御史 - An official position.
  • 河东 - A region in Shanxi Province.
  • 长安 - The capital city of the Sui Dynasty, now Xi'an.
  • 长乐坡 - A specific location.
  • 程雄 - A person's name.
  • 鹦鹉 - A person's name.

[1479]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 無傲，粗暴丈夫也 - Introduces the character Wu Ao, emphasizing his temperament as a rough and violent man.
  • 遂將鹦鹉游行数岁 - The phrase '游行' is used here to mean 'traveling' or 'wandering' for several years.
  • 忽尔见留 - The phrase '忽尔' conveys the suddenness of being detained.
  • 不意遭逢天镜，隐形无路 - The phrase '遭逢' means 'to encounter,' and '隐形无路' means 'no way to hide.'
  • 汝本老狐，变形为人 - The phrase '变形为人' means 'transforming into a human.'
  • 岂不害人也 - The phrase '岂不' is a rhetorical question meaning 'how could it not be the case that.'
  • 变形事人，非有害也 - The phrase '事人' means 'to serve people.'
  • 但逃匿幻惑，神道所恶，自当至死耳 - The phrase '逃匿幻惑' means 'escaping and hiding through illusion,' and '神道所恶' means 'detested by the divine.'
  • 欲舍汝可乎 - The phrase '欲舍汝' means 'to let you go.'
  • 辱公厚赐，岂敢忘德 - The phrase '辱公厚赐' means 'humbled by your generous gifts.'
  • 羞复故体 - The phrase '羞复故体' means 'ashamed to return to my original form.'
  • 愿缄于匣 - The phrase '缄于匣' means 'sealed in a box.'
  • 许尽醉而终 - The phrase '尽醉而终' means 'drink to my heart's content and die.'
  • 缄镜于匣，汝不逃乎 - The phrase '缄镜于匣' means 'seal the mirror in the box.'
  • 公适有美言，尚许相舍 - The phrase '适有美言' means 'just said something nice.'
  • 惟希数刻之命，以尽一生之欢耳 - The phrase '数刻之命' means 'a few moments of life,' and '尽一生之欢' means 'enjoy the rest of my life.'
  • 度即匣镜致酒，悉召雄家邻里与宴 - The phrase '致酒' means 'prepared wine,' and '悉召' means 'invited all.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 无傲，粗暴丈夫也 - Wu Ao is described as a rough and violent man, a common character type in classical Chinese literature.
  • 鹦鹉 - Parrots were often used in classical Chinese stories as companions or messengers.
  • 天镜 - The Heavenly Mirror is a mythical object that can reveal hidden truths or forms, often used in classical Chinese tales.
  • 神道所恶 - The divine disapproves of certain actions, reflecting the moral and spiritual beliefs of the time.
  • 缄于匣 - Sealing something in a box is a metaphor for containment or confinement, often used in stories involving magical or supernatural elements.

[1480]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 宝镜宝镜，哀哉予命 - A lamentation, 'Ah, my fate!' or 'Alas, my life!'
  • 自我离形，于今几姓 - A reflection on the passage of time and the changing of generations.
  • 生虽可乐，死必不伤 - A stoic acceptance of mortality, 'Life may be enjoyable, but death will not harm me.'
  • 何为眷恋，守此一方 - A desire for detachment and transcendence, 'Why cling to this place, this one spot?'
  • 歌讫，再拜，化为老狸而死 - A supernatural transformation, 'After singing, he bowed twice, and then transformed into an old fox and died.'
  • 大业八年四月一日，太阳亏 - A specific date and event, 'On the first day of April, in the eighth year of the Great Prosperity era, the sun was eclipsed.'
  • 度引镜，镜亦昏昧 - A parallel action, 'The man took out the mirror, and the mirror became dim.'
  • 俄而光彩渐出，日亦渐明 - A poetic description of light emerging, 'Soon, its light gradually emerged, and the sun also became brighter.'
  • 每月蚀亦然 - A recurring event, 'The same happened during each lunar eclipse.'
  • 其年八月十五日，友人薛侠者获一铜剑 - A specific date and event, 'On the fifteenth day of August in that year, a friend named Xue Xia obtained a copper sword.'
  • 长四尺，剑连于把 - A detailed description, 'It was four feet long, with the blade connected to the hilt.'
  • 把盘龙凤之状，左文如火焰，右文如水波，光彩灼烁 - An ornate description, 'The hilt was shaped like a coiled dragon and phoenix, with patterns on the left resembling flames and on the right resembling waves, and it was brightly radiant.'
  • 侠持过度曰 - A direct quote, 'Xue Xia held the sword up to the mirror and said...'
  • 此剑，侠常试之，每月十五日，天地清朗，置之暗室，自然有光，旁照数丈 - A detailed description of the sword's properties, 'This sword, I have often tested it. On the fifteenth day of each month, when the sky is clear, if placed in a dark room, it naturally emits light, illuminating several feet around it.'
  • 侠持之有日月矣 - A metaphor, 'Xue Xia held it, and it had the light of the sun and moon.'
  • 明公好奇爱古，愿与君一试 - An invitation, 'Sir, you are fond of antiquities and curiosities. I wish to share this with you.'
  • 度喜甚 - A reaction, 'The man was very pleased.'
  • 其夜密闭一室，无复脱隙 - A detailed preparation, 'That night, they sealed a room tightly, leaving no gaps.'
  • 与侠同宿 - A shared experience, 'They stayed together in the room.'
  • 度亦出宝镜，置于座侧 - A parallel action, 'The man also took out the magical mirror and placed it beside him.'
  • 俄而镜上吐光，明照一室 - A poetic description of light emerging, 'Soon, the mirror emitted light, brightly illuminating the entire room.'
  • 相视如昼 - A vivid metaphor, 'They could see each other as if it were daytime.'
  • 剑横其侧，无复光彩 - A contrast, 'The sword lay beside it, without any more light.'
  • 侠大惊曰 - A reaction, 'Xue Xia was greatly surprised and said...'
  • 请内镜于匣 - A request, 'Please put the mirror back in its box.'
  • 度从其言 - An action, 'The man followed his advice.'
  • 然后剑乃吐光，不过一二尺耳 - A poetic description of light emerging, 'Then the sword began to emit light, but only a few feet.'
  • 侠抚剑叹 - A reaction, 'Xue Xia touched the sword and sighed.'
  • 若月影入室，则无光也 - A condition, 'If the moon's shadow entered the room, there would be no light.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 大业八年 - The eighth year of the reign of Emperor Yang of Sui (589-618 CE).
  • 宝镜 - A magical mirror, often associated with supernatural powers in Chinese folklore.
  • 铜剑 - A copper sword, possibly an ancient artifact with mystical properties.
  • 薛侠 - A friend of the protagonist, likely a historical or fictional figure.
  • 月蚀 - Lunar eclipse, a natural phenomenon with cultural significance in ancient China.
  • 月望 - Full moon, a significant time in Chinese culture, often associated with festivals and rituals.

Proper Nouns

  • 大业八年 - The eighth year of the reign of Emperor Yang of Sui.
  • 宝镜 - A magical mirror.
  • 铜剑 - A copper sword.
  • 薛侠 - A friend of the protagonist.
  • 月蚀 - Lunar eclipse.
  • 月望 - Full moon.

[1481]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 大业九年正月朔旦 - First day of the first month in the ninth year of the Da Ye era, a specific date in the Sui Dynasty.
  • 胡僧行乞而至 - A foreign monk who came to beg for alms.
  • 度弟见之，觉其神采不俗 - The younger brother of the family noticed the monk's extraordinary demeanor.
  • 檀越 - A respectful term for a benefactor or donor.
  • 宝镜 - Refers to a precious mirror.
  • 碧光连日，绛气属月 - Describes the mirror's aura as a continuous green light during the day and a red glow at night.
  • 金膏涂之，珠粉拭之 - Means to apply gold paste and rub with pearl powder.
  • 照日，必影彻墙壁 - Means that when used to reflect sunlight, the mirror's image will penetrate walls.
  • 藏之泥中，亦不晦矣 - Means that if buried in mud, the mirror will not lose its luster.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 大业九年 - The ninth year of the Da Ye era, which was during the reign of Emperor Yang of Sui (581-618).
  • 胡僧 - Refers to a foreign monk, often associated with Buddhist traditions from India or Central Asia.
  • 宝镜 - In Chinese culture, mirrors were often seen as magical objects with special properties.
  • 金膏 - Gold paste, a precious material used for various purposes including decoration and medicine.
  • 珠粉 - Pearl powder, another precious material used for similar purposes.

Proper Nouns

  • 大业九年 - The ninth year of the Da Ye era.
  • 胡僧 - A foreign monk.
  • 宝镜 - A precious mirror.

[1482]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 其年秋 - Means 'that autumn' or 'that fall,' indicating a specific time frame.
  • 度出兼芮城令 - Means 'Du was appointed as the magistrate of Ruicheng County.' The term '出兼' suggests a temporary or additional appointment.
  • 令厅前有一枣树 - Means 'in front of the magistrate's hall, there was a jujube tree.' The phrase '令厅前' specifies the location.
  • 围可数丈 - Means 'its circumference was several zhang (a traditional Chinese unit of length).' The term '可' indicates an approximate measurement.
  • 不知几百年矣 - Means 'it has been there for several hundred years.' The term '几百年' is an indefinite quantity.

  • 迢迢隔千里，各在天一方 - Formal expression meaning 'Separated by thousands of miles, each in a different corner of the sky,' emphasizing the vast distance between the speaker and the person they are addressing.
  • 欲飞恨无翼，欲涉川无梁 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'Wish to fly but lack wings, wish to cross a river but lack a bridge,' symbolizing unfulfilled desires and obstacles.
  • 昔者面胶漆，今胡做参商 - Reference to the ancient Chinese idioms '面胶漆' (face like glue and lacquer, meaning inseparable) and '参商' (constellations that never appear together, meaning estrangement), contrasting past closeness with current distance.
  • 平平长安道，人马自辉光 - Poetic description meaning 'On the level path of Chang'an, people and horses shine with their own radiance,' evoking a scene of prosperity and activity.
  • 不念莫逆好，虚名竟乖张 - Moral critique meaning 'Forget the deep friendship, and the empty fame becomes distorted,' suggesting a betrayal of true values for superficial recognition.
  • 南箕岂堪簸，牵牛难服箱 - Metaphorical expression using constellations 'Nanji' (southern asterism) and 'Qian Niu' (the oxen driver), meaning 'The southern asterism is not fit for winnowing, the oxen driver cannot harness the carriage,' symbolizing mismatched roles or abilities.
  • 谅无金石心，与君永相忘 - Metaphorical expression meaning 'I know we lack the heart of gold and stone, and will forever forget each other,' expressing the fragility of human emotions and the inevitability of forgetting.
  • 已而鸡三唱，女闻之，这遽披衣，谓纲曰 - Narrative expression meaning 'Afterward, when the rooster crowed three times, the woman heard it and quickly put on her clothes, saying to Gāng,' introducing a shift in the narrative and dialogue.
  • 郎君珍重，明当重来，不待请矣 - Formal expression meaning 'Take care, dear sir, I will come again tomorrow, without needing to be invited,' showing the woman's concern and initiative.
  • 纲执意留之，曰 - Narrative expression meaning 'Gāng insisted on keeping her, saying,' indicating the man's determination and the continuation of the dialogue.
  • 只此自匿，奚必去耶 - Rhetorical question meaning 'Why leave when you can hide here?' expressing the man's desire to keep the woman with him.
  • 家有父母，倘事败露，罪将安归 - Moral concern meaning 'We have parents at home, if the matter is exposed, where will the blame fall?' highlighting the potential consequences of their actions.
  • 不惟有玷于妾，抑且不利于君 - Moral critique meaning 'It will not only tarnish me, but also harm you,' emphasizing the mutual risks involved.
  • 纲不从，女力奔，纲以被裹而抱之 - Narrative expression meaning 'Gāng did not comply, the woman ran with all her might, and Gāng wrapped her in a quilt and held her,' describing a physical struggle.
  • 久之不动，及启视，则一砧杵也 - Narrative expression meaning 'After a long time, she did not move, and when he looked, it was a pestle,' revealing the surprising transformation.
  • 天宝元年七月七日，真如于精舍户外，盥濯之间，忽有五色云气自东而来 - Narrative expression meaning 'On the seventh day of the seventh month in the first year of Tianbao, Zhenru was washing outside the temple when suddenly a five-colored cloud came from the east,' introducing a supernatural event.
  • 云中引手，不见其形，徐以囊授真如曰 - Narrative expression meaning 'From the cloud, a hand appeared, unseen, and slowly handed a bag to Zhenru, saying,' describing the mysterious interaction.
  • 宝之，慎勿言也 - Formal instruction meaning 'Keep it safe, do not speak of it,' emphasizing the importance of secrecy.
  • 真如谨守，不敢失坠 - Formal expression meaning 'Zhenru kept it carefully, not daring to lose it,' showing her dedication to the task.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 翌日 - Means 'the next day' or 'the following day.'

  • 安宜县有妖尼之事，怪之甚也 - '安宜县' is the name of the county, '妖尼' means 'evil nun,' '怪之甚也' means 'found it very strange.'
  • 亟往讯之 - '亟' means 'hastily' or 'immediately,' '往讯之' means 'to go and interrogate her.'
  • 宝非人力所致，又何疑焉 - '宝非人力所致' means 'the treasure was not created by human effort,' '又何疑焉' means 'what more can be doubted?'
  • 乃以囊中五宝示恒 - '乃' means 'then,' '以囊中五宝示恒' means 'then showed the five treasures in the bag to Lu Heng.'
  • 玄黄天符 - '玄黄' means 'black and yellow,' '天符' means 'heavenly talisman.'
  • 形如笏，长可八寸余，阔三寸，上圆下方，近圆有孔，黄玉也 - '形如笏' means 'shaped like a tablet,' '长可八寸余' means 'about eight inches long,' '阔三寸' means 'three inches wide,' '上圆下方' means 'round at the top and square at the bottom,' '近圆有孔' means 'with a hole near the top,' '黄玉也' means 'made of yellow jade.'
  • 色比蒸栗，润若凝脂，避人间兵疫邪疡 - '色比蒸栗' means 'the color is like steamed chestnuts,' '润若凝脂' means 'smooth like congealed fat,' '避人间兵疫邪疡' means 'averts human warfare, epidemics, and diseases.'
  • 玉鸡，毛文悉备，白玉也 - '玉鸡' means 'jade chicken,' '毛文悉备' means 'with all feathers complete,' '白玉也' means 'made of white jade.'
  • 王者以孝理天下，则见 - '王者' means 'ruler,' '以孝理天下' means 'governs the world with filial piety,' '则见' means 'then it appears.'
  • 谷壁，白玉也 - '谷壁' means 'grain wall,' '白玉也' means 'made of white jade.'
  • 径五六寸，其文粟粒自生，无异雕镌之状 - '径五六寸' means 'about five to six inches in diameter,' '其文粟粒自生' means 'with grain-like patterns naturally formed,' '无异雕镌之状' means 'no different from carved patterns.'
  • 玉珍，有两枚，亦白玉也 - '玉珍' means 'jade treasure,' '有两枚' means 'there are two pieces,' '亦白玉也' means 'also made of white jade.'
  • 好倍于肉 - '好倍于肉' means 'the quality is twice that of ordinary jade.'
  • 王者得之，能令外国归服 - '王者得之' means 'when the ruler obtains it,' '能令外国归服' means 'can make foreign countries submit.'
  • 玉色光彩溢发，特异于常 - '玉色光彩溢发' means 'the jade shines with a radiant light,' '特异于常' means 'especially extraordinary.'
  • 真如乃悉出宝盘，向空照之 - '真如乃悉出宝盘' means 'Zhenru then took out the treasure plate,' '向空照之' means 'and held it up to the sky to shine.'
  • 其光皆射日，仰望，不知光之所极也 - '其光皆射日' means 'the light all shone towards the sun,' '仰望，不知光之所极也' means 'looking up, one could not see where the light ended.'
  • 恒与县吏同视，咸异之 - '恒与县吏同视' means 'Lu Heng and the county officials looked together,' '咸异之' means 'all found it strange.'

  • The clause '群仙喜曰：“君不闻刘阮事乎？今日亦如是。愿奉一醉，将尽春色。君以为何如？”' must be read as a single speech from the immortals, introducing their welcoming toast.
  • The clause '谢曰：“不意今日得为刘阮，幸何甚哉。然则次为何所，女郎又何为者，愿一闻知。”' must be read as a single response from the protagonist, expressing his surprise and seeking information.
  • The long clause '我玉清之女也，居于此久矣。此乃玉清宫也。向闻君为下第进士，寓游至此，将以一言奉请，又惧君子不顾，且贻其辱，是人假郑氏之亭以命君，果副吾志。虽然，此仙府也。虽云不可滞，世间人君居之，固无损耳。' must be read as a single explanation from the immortals, providing background and context.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 旬余 - Means 'about ten days' or 'a little over a week,' indicating a short period of time.
  • 群胡 - Refers to a group of barbarians or non-Han Chinese people, often used in historical contexts to describe nomadic tribes or foreigners.
  • 持金缯为寿 - Means 'bringing gold and silk as gifts for longevity,' a common practice in ancient China where gifts were given to wish someone a long life.
  • 固拒之 - Means 'firmly refuse them,' indicating a strong resistance to the gifts.
  • 吾子居长安中，惶惶然有饥寒色 - A polite way of saying 'you live in Chang'an, looking hungry and cold,' where '吾子' is a respectful term for 'you' or 'sir.'
  • 所以交吾子欢耳 - Means 'it's just to make you happy,' indicating the purpose of the gift.
  • 无有他渎 - Means 'there is no other intention,' emphasizing that the gift is purely friendly.
  • 太学中诸生闻之 - Means 'the students at the Imperial Academy heard about it,' referring to the students studying at the national university.
  • 彼胡率爱利，不顾其身 - Means 'those barbarians are usually greedy and don't care about themselves,' criticizing the behavior of the barbarians.
  • 争盐菜之微，尚致相贼杀者 - Means 'they even fight over small things like salt and vegetables, leading to mutual slaughter,' highlighting the barbarians' savagery.
  • 宁肯弃金缯为子寿乎 - Means 'would they really give up gold and silk as gifts for your longevity?' questioning the sincerity of their offer.
  • 何为独厚君耶 - Means 'why are they treating you so specially?' expressing surprise at the special treatment.
  • 是必有故 - Means 'there must be a reason,' suggesting that there is an underlying motive.
  • 君宜匿身郊野间，以避之也 - Means 'you should hide yourself in the suburbs to avoid them,' advising caution.
  • 侨居渭上 - Means 'live in exile on the banks of the Wei River,' indicating a change in residence.
  • 杜门不出 - Means 'shut the door and do not go out,' describing a reclusive lifestyle.
  • 群胡又诣其门 - Means 'the group of barbarians came to his door again,' indicating a return visit.
  • 我未得尽言 - Means 'I couldn't fully explain myself,' suggesting that there was something left unsaid.
  • 今君退居郊野，果吾心也 - Means 'now that you have retreated to the suburbs, it is exactly what I wanted,' expressing satisfaction.
  • 我之来，非偶然也，盖有求君耳 - Means 'my coming here is not accidental; I have something to ask of you,' indicating a specific purpose.
  • 幸望诺之 - Means 'I hope you will agree,' making a request.
  • 且我所祈，于君，固无害；于我，则大惠也 - Means 'what I ask of you is harmless to you but very beneficial to me,' explaining the mutual benefit.
  • 谨受教 - Means 'I will follow your advice,' showing willingness to comply.
  • 吾子好食面乎 - Means 'do you like eating noodles?' asking about a personal preference.
  • 食面者非君也，乃君腹中一虫耳 - Means 'the one who eats noodles is not you, but a worm in your stomach,' a metaphorical statement.
  • 今我欲以一粒药进君，君饵之，当吐出虫，则我以厚价从君易之，其可乎 - Means 'now I want to give you a pill. If you take it, you will vomit out the worm, and I will pay a high price to buy it from you,' proposing a deal.

  • 忽有一童，分发，衣青襦，自海水中出，捧白盘。盘中有径寸珠甚多，来献胡人 - Suddenly, a boy appeared, with parted hair, wearing a green robe, emerging from the sea water, carrying a white tray. The tray contained many pearls about an inch in diameter, which he presented to the Hu people.

  • 俄有一仙人，戴碧瑶冠，衣紫霞衣，捧绛帕籍盘，中有一珠，径二寸许，奇光泛彩，照数十步，仙人以献胡人，胡人笑而受之，喜谓曰：“至宝来矣。” - Soon, a fairy appeared, wearing a green jade crown and a purple mist robe, carrying a red silk tray containing a pearl about two inches in diameter, radiating a wondrous light that illuminated dozens of steps. The fairy presented it to the Hu people, who laughed and accepted it, happily saying, 'The ultimate treasure has arrived.'

  • 几度乘风上九天 - Several times riding the wind to the ninth heaven; '乘风' means 'riding the wind,' and '九天' refers to the highest heavens in Chinese cosmology.
  • 长夜听琴来蕙帐 - Listening to the lute in the long night in the fragrant tent; '蕙帐' refers to a tent fragrant with orchids, often associated with immortals.
  • 清晨觅食在芝田 - Seeking food in the morning in the orchid fields; '芝田' refers to fields of orchids, symbolizing a paradise or the realm of immortals.

  • 沧海桑田几变迁 - How many changes have occurred in the sea and fields; '沧海桑田' is a metaphor for great changes over time.

  • 君行宜珍重，勿轻回顾 - You should be careful on your journey, do not lightly look back; '珍重' means 'to be careful,' and '勿轻回顾' means 'do not lightly look back.'
  • 始忆诗赞为鹤焉 - Only then did he remember that the poem praised a crane; '始忆' means 'only then did he remember,' and '鹤' means 'crane.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 于立义里居 - Resided in Liyi Street, where '于' (yú) means 'in' and '居' (jū) means 'to live.'
  • 见武士数百骑 - Saw hundreds of mounted warriors, where '骑' (qí) means 'mounted' or 'riding.'
  • 升自床之西南隅 - Ascended from the southwest corner of the bed, where '升' (shēng) means 'ascend' or 'climb.'
  • 于花坛上置缯缴 - Set up silk nooses on the flower bed, where '于' (yú) means 'on' and '置' (zhì) means 'to place.'
  • 纵兵大猎 - Sent the soldiers to hunt extensively, where '纵' (zòng) means 'to send' or 'to let loose.'
  • 猎讫 - After the hunt, where '讫' (qì) means 'completed' or 'finished.'
  • 有旌旗豹纛，并导骑数百 - There were flags and leopard banners, along with hundreds of leading cavalry, where '并' (bìng) means 'and' or 'together with.'
  • 又自外入至西北隅 - Also entered from the outside to the northwest corner, where '又' (yòu) means 'also' or 'further.'
  • 有带剑操斧，手执槌镬者 - There were those wearing swords and axes, holding hammers and pots, where '操' (cāo) means 'to wield' or 'to hold.'
  • 挈幄幕帘榻、盘碟鼎筐者 - Carrying tents, curtains, tables, plates, pots, and baskets, where '挈' (qiè) means 'to carry.'
  • 负器盛陆海之珍味者 - Carrying containers filled with rare delicacies from land and sea, where '负' (fù) means 'to carry' and '盛' (shèng) means 'to contain.'
  • 道路往返，奔走探侦者 - Those who ran back and forth, exploring and investigating, where '探侦' (tàn zhēn) means 'to explore' or 'to investigate.'
  • 至中军 - To the central army, where '中军' (zhōng jūn) refers to the main body of the army.
  • 拥帻帧紫衣者 - One holding a purple robe and a headband, where '拥' (yōng) means 'to hold' and '帻' (zé) means 'headband.'
  • 至案之右 - To the right of the table, where '案' (àn) means 'table' or 'bench.'
  • 大铁冠执钺前宣言曰 - Wearing a large iron crown and holding a halberd, he declared in front, where '前宣言' (qián xuān yán) means 'to declare in front.'
  • 殿下将欲观渔于紫石潭 - The prince will go fishing at the Purple Stone Pool, where '殿下' (diàn xià) means 'prince' or 'royal prince.'
  • 其先锋后军及甲士执戈朝勿从 - The front and rear guards, as well as the armored soldiers holding spears, should not follow, where '勿从' (wù cóng) means 'do not follow.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The long sequence '已而夜静酒阑，彼此忘怀，笑语欢谑，挨肩携手，淫情各炽，遂入室解衣就寝，云情雨意，两相欢合，口送丁香，极尽绸缪' describes a series of actions and emotions, leading to a sexual encounter. The syntax is fluid and continuous, capturing the progression of events and feelings.
  • The placement of '一律' (a single poem) at the end of the sentence in '美人就枕上吟诗一律' is somewhat ambiguous but suggests that the woman recites a single poem.
  • The metaphorical expression '尽付湘江水上沤' in '追思向日繁华地，尽付湘江水上沤' is rich in imagery but requires careful translation to convey the intended meaning of the transient nature of life.
  • The use of '祸起萧墙' in '本欲与君共期偕老，不料上天降罚，祸起萧墙' adds a layer of complexity to the statement, indicating that the tragedy is caused by internal conflict.
  • The contrast between the present and the future in '今夕尽此一欢，明朝永别' is clear but requires a translation that captures the emotional weight of the statement.
  • The conditional structure in '君宜速避，不然祸且及君' is straightforward but the urgency and gravity of the warning must be conveyed effectively.

[1674]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 不然 - A common phrase meaning 'if not so' or 'otherwise,' used to introduce a counter-argument.
  • 渭之钓叟，傅之筑佣 - A metaphorical expression referring to historical figures: 'Wang Yi' (渭之钓叟), a fisherman by the Wei River, and 'Fu Yue' (傅之筑佣), a laborer who built walls. These figures are used to represent humble origins.
  • 拔茅而物色 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'to select or choose carefully,' often used in the context of selecting talent.
  • 一竿一版，朽烂滨岩之下 - A vivid description of a fisherman's pole and a laborer's tool being left to rot near the rocks, symbolizing a life of obscurity and neglect.
  • 老死无闻矣 - A phrase meaning 'to die old and unknown,' emphasizing the fate of those who are not recognized or remembered.
  • 遇又不可强也 - A statement meaning 'encounter cannot be forced,' suggesting that opportunities must be seized when they arise.
  • 信如子言 - A phrase meaning 'if what you say is true,' used to introduce a hypothetical scenario.
  • 甘与庸庸者伍 - A phrase meaning 'to willingly associate with the mediocre,' expressing a desire to avoid such associations.
  • 杏园一宴，桃李春官 - A metaphorical expression referring to a prestigious gathering or event, often associated with success and recognition.
  • 南柯梦后 - A reference to the 'South柯 Dream,' a Chinese folktale about a man who experiences a brief but glorious life before waking to find it was all a dream, symbolizing the fleeting nature of success.
  • 衰草荒棒，寒烟暮雨 - A vivid description of desolate and bleak conditions, often used to symbolize the end of a prosperous life.
  • 同一丘耳 - A phrase meaning 'all end up in the same grave,' emphasizing the equality of death regardless of one's status.
  • 孰分梧棘乎 - A rhetorical question meaning 'who can distinguish between good and bad?' highlighting the futility of trying to differentiate between different outcomes.
  • 世之急功名者何限 - A phrase meaning 'how many people in the world are eager for fame and success,' emphasizing the widespread desire for achievement.
  • 忤众者愿我 - A phrase meaning 'those who oppose the crowd wish for me,' suggesting a contrast between the majority and the individual.
  • 妾非愿君，欲悟君耳 - A phrase meaning 'I am not wishing for you, but rather trying to awaken you,' expressing the speaker's intention to enlighten rather than simply wish for something.
  • 垂涎富贵者，不啻望梅止渴 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'those who covet wealth and status are no different from those who hope for plums to quench their thirst,' emphasizing the unrealistic and futile nature of such desires.
  • 妄想功名者，孰无松梦之思 - A phrase meaning 'those who foolishly dream of fame and success, who does not have such thoughts?' highlighting the commonality of such aspirations.
  • 攘攘营营，争枝匝树 - A vivid description of people bustling and striving, often used to depict the competitive nature of society.
  • 忙逐槐尘而不惜，祸甘桃实而莫知 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'they rush after the dust of the Chinese scholar tree without hesitation, and suffer the consequences of the sweet peach without knowing,' emphasizing the blindness and recklessness of pursuing success.
  • 方将宴笑堂中，而长夜之室，人已为我筑矣 - A vivid description of a person who is still enjoying success, but whose tomb has already been prepared for them, symbolizing the sudden and unexpected nature of death.
  • 悲思此景，愿将何属乎 - A phrase meaning 'to grieve over this scene, what can one wish for?' expressing the futility of wishing for anything in the face of such a bleak reality.
  • 人孰无死也 - A phrase meaning 'who among us does not die?' a simple and universal truth about mortality.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 因悟其言，改名希靖，不求仕进，归家享年寿云 - Means 'because of understanding his words, he changed his name to Xi Jing, did not seek official positions, and returned home to enjoy a long life,' indicating a personal transformation and retreat from public life.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 仲冬 - Refers to the middle of winter, typically the twelfth month in the traditional Chinese lunar calendar.
  • 二七 - Refers to the seventh day of the second month, a specific date in the lunar calendar.
  • 江上 - Refers to the river, often associated with literary and romantic imagery in Chinese poetry.
  • 临歧 - Refers to parting, a common theme in Chinese poetry and literature.
  • 千里还相思 - A common poetic expression indicating deep longing over great distances.
  • 妖柳 - Refers to a demon willow, a supernatural or deceptive entity in Chinese folklore.
  • 花木之事 - Refers to matters related to flowers and trees, often used in classical Chinese literature to denote refined or scholarly topics.
  • 凿凿造理 - Refers to being firmly based on reason, indicating logical argumentation.
  • 希靖 - A name that indicates a desire for peace and tranquility.
  • 仕进 - Refers to seeking official positions or advancement in government.
  • 享年寿 - Refers to enjoying a long life, a common wish in Chinese culture.

Proper Nouns

  • 薛藩 - The name of the person being discussed.
  • 薛 - The surname of the person.
  • 河东 - Refers to the region of He Dong, a historical area in northern China.
  • 希靖 - The new name adopted by the person.
